user_query:
I need actionable follow-ups and reminders from our latest design review. Using the two inputs below, please produce four outputs under out/ that I can drop into my workflow.

Inputs:
- input/meeting_raw_notes.txt
- input/project_backlog.csv

Deliverables:
1) out/meeting_minutes.md — Summarize the meeting into clear minutes with two sections: "Decisions" and "Action items". Parse ACTION lines from input/meeting_raw_notes.txt and list each action with id, owner, due date (ISO), related_task_id, and text. Include the DECISION lines verbatim under "Decisions". If any ACTION owner disagrees with the owner of the corresponding related_task_id in input/project_backlog.csv, add a short "Notes" subsection listing those mismatches. Otherwise omit the "Notes" subsection.

2) out/ranked_followups.csv — From input/project_backlog.csv, filter rows where tag == "eigensolver" AND status == "open". Sort deterministically by: priority ascending, then impact descending, then effort ascending, then id ascending. Write the same columns as the input plus an extra column "rank" (1..n in the sorted order).

3) out/status_update.md — Compose a concise status update for the team that includes: (a) the meeting date from input/meeting_raw_notes.txt, (b) a one-sentence recap of the decisions, (c) a bullet list of the top 3 items from out/ranked_followups.csv (each bullet must include id, title, owner, and deadline if present, otherwise "no deadline"), and (d) the count of open eigensolver items (from the filtered set used in ranked_followups). Keep it short and informative.

4) out/reminders.tsv — Prepare follow-up reminders as a TSV with columns: due_date, owner, message, related_task_id. Include one row per ACTION from input/meeting_raw_notes.txt (message = the ACTION text), and also one row for each of the top 3 items in out/ranked_followups.csv that have a non-empty deadline (message = "Follow up on task <id>: <title>"). Use ISO dates from the inputs; do not invent dates.

All outputs must be derived solely from the provided inputs, and sorting must follow the specified rules exactly so the results are reproducible. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
